common.deeplUnavailable: DeepL service is temporarily unreachable, please check the related settings in API Settings.
common.llmUnavailable: LLM service is temporarily unreachable, please check the related settings in API Settings.
common.noFileUploaded: Please upload a file to process first.
common.sourceTextPlaceholder: Enter or paste text to process...
common.noSourceText: Please enter or paste text content.
common.formatTooltip: Format: remove blank lines and trim leading/trailing spaces
common.resultToSourceTooltip: Overwrite input area with result text
common.readOnlyModeTooltip: Text is too long: the editor is temporarily read-only and shows a partial preview only. Your full content is unaffected — processing and export still use the entire text.
common.clearInputTooltip: Clear input content and uploaded files
common.removeCharsAfterTranslationTooltip: Automatically remove specified characters after translation (separate multiple with spaces)
common.multiLangSettingsHint: Enter language codes, separated by comma or space.
common.retryCountTooltip: Number of retry attempts when translation fails
common.requestTimeoutSecTooltip: Maximum wait per request (seconds). LLM thinking takes longer; 180s or more recommended.
common.customExportFilenameTooltip: '{'name'}' = original filename, '{'lang'}' = language, '{'ext'}' = extension, '{'date'}' = date, '{'time'}' = time
common.partialFailureDesc: Failed lines kept the original text as a placeholder, so the file stays usable. These failures are usually network blips or temporary upstream hiccups — free services and self-hosted servers are especially prone to this, and it's not a problem with your setup. Click Retry to continue (successful lines come from cache, no re-translation); if it still fails, try again later or switch to a different translation service. Or click View failed lines to handle them manually.
common.failedLanguagesDesc: Every batch for these languages errored out — usually local LLM glitches, API rate limits, or the model refusing a specific language. Copy the codes and re-run targeting only them (paste into Quick Entry via Language Codes); the others will hit cache and skip.
common.gtxFreeAPIUnavailable: GTX Free service is temporarily unreachable — could be a network restriction. Please try again later, or switch to a different translation service.
common.translategemmaUnavailable: TranslateGemma service is temporarily unreachable, please make sure your local server (LM Studio / llama.cpp / Ollama) is running and the URL is correct.
common.networkUnavailable: Translation service is temporarily unreachable — could be a network issue or the service is having a hiccup. Please try again later, or switch to a different translation service.
common.translationTimeout: Translation request timed out — could be a slow network or the service is taking too long to respond. Please try again later, or switch to a different translation service.

textSplitter.title: Text Splitter
textSplitter.description: Smart long text splitter for AI and social media
textSplitter.metaTitle: Text / TXT File Splitter for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Twitter
textSplitter.metaDescription: Split long text or TXT files for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Twitter, Weibo by char count, paragraph, or custom delimiter. GBK/Shift-JIS auto.

faq.q: What are the character limits when splitting text for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and translation tools?
faq.a: AI model context limits (tokens): ChatGPT GPT-5 ~400K, Claude Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.7 1M, Gemini 3 Pro 1M, DeepSeek V4 1M. Chinese is ~1-2 chars/token (Claude more generous), English ~4 chars/token. Translation limits: Google Translate and DeepL free both 5000 chars/request. Set 'Split Character Count' with buffer below the target AI's token capacity.

faq.q: How do I split long posts for Twitter/X, Threads, Mastodon, or Bluesky?
faq.a: Platform limits: Twitter/X 280 chars, Threads 500, Mastodon 500, Bluesky 300, LinkedIn 3000, Tumblr 4096, Reddit posts 40000, Substack notes 500. Use 'English paragraph' mode for Latin scripts — preserves sentence integrity at word boundaries. Batch-export as separate files for sequential posting (1/N, 2/N numbering).

faq.q: Once the chunks are split, what tools pair well for translating or further cleaning them?
faq.a: If the chunks are i18n JSON fragments (a language file split by key), use [JSON Translator](../json-translate) to batch-translate while preserving keys. For Markdown long-form (blog posts split by chapter), use [Markdown Translator](../md-translator) — it preserves code blocks and Front Matter. For heavier cleanup (dedup, regex extraction, URL pulling) before feeding chunks to AI, run them through the [Text Toolbox](../text-toolbox) first.

faq.q: How do I handle mixed Chinese/English text or non-UTF-8 files (French GBK, Japanese Shift-JIS)?
faq.a: For mixed CJK/English, use Chinese paragraph mode — it recognizes both Chinese (。！？) and English (. ! ?) sentence endings without splitting mid-word. Custom delimiter supports escape chars \\n\\n and \\t. For non-UTF-8 files (French GBK, Japanese Shift-JIS, Korean EUC-KR), **upload the file directly** rather than pasting — encoding is auto-detected to prevent garbled output.

faq.q: Is my text uploaded to a server? Is the tool open source?
faq.a: 100% client-side: text reading, splitting, and export all happen in your browser — nothing is uploaded to any server, so sensitive content is safe. Source code is open on GitHub (rockbenben/text-splitter) for self-hosting or auditing.

howto.name: Input long text into the tool
howto.text: Paste very long text (novel chapters, speeches, research papers) or upload TXT file. For non-UTF-8 files (French GBK, Japanese Shift-JIS, Korean EUC-KR), upload the file directly for auto encoding detection.

howto.name: Set character count by AI model or platform
howto.text: ChatGPT free 16K, Plus 32K, Pro 128K, Claude 200K, Gemini 1M; Twitter/X 280, Threads 500, Mastodon 500, Bluesky 300, LinkedIn 3000. Configure character count with buffer below the target limit.

howto.name: Export for AI input or social posting
howto.text: Copy single segments directly into ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini. Batch-export as separate files numbered 1/N, 2/N for sequential posting on Twitter/Threads/Mastodon/LinkedIn. Merged export for saving organized long-form text.

features: AI model contexts: GPT-5 400K, Claude 4.6/4.7 1M, Gemini 3 Pro 1M, DeepSeek V4 1M
Social platform templates: Twitter 280, Threads 500, Mastodon 500, Bluesky 300, LinkedIn 3000
Smart punctuation detection for mixed CJK (。！？) and Latin (. ! ?) text
Non-UTF-8 auto-detection (GBK, Shift-JIS, EUC-KR) prevents garbled output
Custom delimiter with \\n\\n, \\t escape character support
Batch export numbered 1/N, 2/N for sequential posting
